name: tylophora indica
mechanism: Contains tylophorine alkaloids with immunosuppressive, anti-inflammatory, and antiallergic properties.
contraindications: avoid with or in emaciated individuals not for long-term high-dose use
avoid with or in emaciated individuals not for long
safetyNotes: May cause nausea and vomiting in higher doses. gi discomfort is common early in treatment. Avoid in pregnancy; Avoid with with immunosuppressants; Avoid with or in emaciated individuals not for long-term high-dose use; Avoid with or in emaciated individuals not for long; Avoid with term high; Avoid with dose use; Avoid in pregnancy, may cause nausea and vomiting in higher doses; Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, lower dose or stop if nausea, vomiting, or GI upset occurs or nausea and vomiting in higher doses.
description: An ayurvedic herb used for bronchial conditions, immune regulation, and allergic responses.
dosage: 200-400 mg/day extract or 1-2 g dried leaf.
legalStatus: Legal in India; regulated in some countries due to emetic effects
therapeuticUses: and inflammation. sometimes used topically for skin disorders.
sideEffects: nausea and vomiting in higher doses. gi discomfort is common early in treatment.
